metadata.cross_part_loss_rule: Once taxable income in each Part is determined, a negative amount in one Part cannot reduce taxable income in another Part for the surtax.
metadata.negative_part_treatment: Each negative Part is treated as zero
metadata.source_note: Current official DOR guidance, updated June 1, 2026. The retained calculation section publishes the tax-year-2026 $1,107,750 threshold, the 4% excess-income formula, and the rule that the surtax base sums only positive Part A, Part B, and Part C taxable-income amounts after any cross-Part deductions otherwise allowed by statute. This separate guidance scope clarifies the base boundary; it does not certify a complete final-return calculation.
metadata.taxable_income_parts: Part A, Part B, and Part C
metadata.title: Massachusetts 4% Surtax on Taxable Income
